reasoning: The longbow sprite composited into the scene spans roughly 120-130px diagonally. With a 32x32 player reference, weapon items must be 50-75% of player size (~16-24px). The bow is approximately 4x that budget — clearly larger than the player would be. This violates the 'item NEVER larger than player' rule and gives the scene a zoomed-in / prop-showcase feel rather than toy diorama. Perspective angle (diagonal tilt) is acceptable for a top-down 2D context, but the sheer size overwhelms the scene elements and dwarfs the tile grid. The bow needs to be scaled down significantly (target ~20-24px longest dimension) to pass.
